hypothesis: Incorporating molecular charge as a latent conditioning variable into the equivariant message-passing layers of MACE—rather than treating it solely as a scalar input at the readout stage—will yield greater improvements in force prediction accuracy for ionizable organic molecules and charged peptides than charge-neutral pre-training followed by fine-tuning, because charge-dependent polarization effects propagate non-locally through the atomic neighbor density in ways that require charge information to modulate the intermediate equivariant features at each interaction block.
